phrase.text: Good morning. Can I ask you a few questions regarding the watchkeeping duties of a <motorman>?
Ok, my first question is: what does a motorman have to do when he starts his watch?

First of all, he must inspect the engine room, <the steering gear room>, the emergency fire pump compartment and the air conditioning compartment. He must also check the lubricating oil, <the fuel oil sludge> and the water levels of the engine room tanks.
Is checking working and stand-by equipment part of the motorman’s duty?
Yes, of course. And in addition he must also check equipment for abnormal sound and vibrations and check the bilge space for <abnormal leakages>. He must report everything to the duty engineer.
What about his duties during the watch?

A motorman must inspect the engine room and related rooms at regular intervals and keep the engine room clean. If he observes any abnormal leakage, he must report it to <the duty engineer> immediately.
What does the motorman have to do at the end of his watch?
He must <clean> equipment and make sure that the next motorman is ready for duty.
